question_text: In the circle shown below with centre O, chord AB subtends an angle of 100 degrees at the centre. What is the angle subtended by the same chord at a point on the major arc?

answer_key.explanation: The angle subtended at the centre is twice the angle subtended at any point on the major arc, so the answer is half of 100.
common_mistakes: Doubling instead of halving the central angle.
answer_key.related_concept: Angle subtended by a chord at the centre and at the circumference

visual.caption: Circle with chord AB and centre O.
visual.altText: A circle with labelled centre O, a chord AB drawn across the lower half, and a point P marked on the upper arc.
